question.text: What is the maximum trading cycle for exchange-traded currency futures contracts on INR pairs?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency futures contracts on INR pairs (USD-INR, EUR-INR, GBP-INR, JPY-INR) have a maximum of 12 months trading cycle. The expiry day is two working days prior to the last business day of the expiry month at 12:30 PM. All contracts are cash settled. **The Trap:** Long-dated equity index options can have up to 5-year expiry cycles, but currency futures are limited to 12 months. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I covers currency futures contract specifications, FBIL reference rates, and settlement mechanics in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Currency futures: max 12-month cycle, expiry 2 working days before last business day at 12:30 PM, settled at FBIL reference rate. Cross-currency pairs use derived rates from respective FCY-INR rates.
